# MCP Conformance Tests - Base Messaging (JSON-RPC 2.0)
# This test validates the basic JSON-RPC message format requirements for MCP protocol

# Start the test server with minimal capabilities
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# TEST 1: Request Format Requirements
# - Must include jsonrpc: "2.0"
# - Must include a string or integer ID (not null)
# - Must include a method field
# - Params are optional
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"hello"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":1'
stdout '"method":"echo"'
stdout '"params":{"message":"hello"}'

# TEST 2: Response Format Requirements
# - Must include jsonrpc: "2.0"
# - Must include the same ID as the request
# - Must include either result or error, not both
# - Error must include code and message
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"test response"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":2'
stdout '"result":'
! stdout '"error":'

# TEST 3: Error Response Format
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"unknown_method"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":3'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":'
stdout '"message":'
! stdout '"result":'

# TEST 4: Notification Format (no ID)
# - Must include jsonrpc: "2.0"
# - Must include a method field
# - Must NOT include an ID field
# - Params are optional
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/test","params":{"value":"test notification"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
# No response should be received for notifications

# TEST 5: Invalid request - Missing jsonrpc version
setstdin {"id":5,"method":"echo"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32600'
stdout '"message":"Invalid request"'

# TEST 6: Invalid request - Missing method
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":6}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32600'
stdout '"message":"Invalid request"'

# TEST 7: Invalid request - Null ID (not allowed in MCP)
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":null,"method":"echo"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32600'
stdout '"message":"Invalid request"'

# TEST 8: Batch request support
setstdin [{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":801,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"batch1"}},{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":802,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"batch2"}}]
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"id":801'
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"id":802'
stdout '"result":'

# TEST 9: Batch with mixed requests and notifications
setstdin [{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":901,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"batch_req"}},{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/test","params":{"value":"batch_notif"}}]
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"id":901'
stdout '"result":'
# Only the request should get a response, not the notification

# TEST 10: Duplicate request IDs within session (should be rejected)
# First request with ID 1001
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1001,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"first use"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"id":1001'
stdout '"result":'

# Same ID used again in same session (should be rejected)
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1001,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"duplicate id"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"message":"Duplicate request ID"'
